meta.venue: Making contact easier to reason about

sections.title: Constraints - An approach to modeling many physical interactions

blocks.text: A lot of physical tasks become easier to think about when you name the geometry. A drawer follows prismatic motion. A door rotates about a hinge.

figures.caption: Everyday tasks can often be represented as simple constraint patterns.
Simple models can still describe rich interactions.

blocks.caption: Constraint models can easily be enumerated and reasoned about.

items: Simple geometry keeps the model interpretable.
Different tasks can share the same underlying constraint type.
A method for constraint inference using pose and wrench measurements. Subramani, Hagenow, Gleicher, Zinn. arXiv, 2020.
Recognizing geometric constraints in human demonstrations using force and position signals. Subramani, Gleicher, Zinn. IEEE RA-L, 2018.
Inferring Geometric Constraints in Human Demonstrations for Robot Programming. Guru Subramani. PhD Dissertation, 2019.
